https://openalex.org/W4388521516.kf1: CareerAdvance promoted parents' educational advancement over three years, with weaker evidence for career/psychological benefits and no economic gains; effects were larger for parents without baseline postsecondary credentials.

https://openalex.org/W4387167677.kf1: No statistically detectable differences between high- and low-cash groups in maternal assessments of children's health, sleep, or nutrition.

https://openalex.org/W4395693556.kf1: Maternal sensitivity in infancy predicted lower adolescent friend conflict, partially mediated by secure base script knowledge; the script also buffered loneliness under high friend conflict.
https://openalex.org/W4395693556.effect_sizes: Maternal sensitivity->friend conflict β=-0.17; script moderation β=-0.15

https://openalex.org/W4401890623.demographics: Latinx and Zomi immigrant parents and their children
https://openalex.org/W4401890623.kf1: Treatment parents reported higher English writing skills and self-esteem and lower language brokering and material hardship; no significant differences in children's outcomes.

https://openalex.org/W4399215446.kf1: Argues two-generation approaches addressing children's and caregivers' interrelated needs are more effective than single-generation approaches; identifies five global models.

https://openalex.org/W4401077602.kf1: Monthly unconditional cash transfers did not affect mothers' child-directed speech or child vocalizations during home play, though variability was wide.

https://openalex.org/W4399049405.demographics: Youth and advisors in gender-sexuality alliances
https://openalex.org/W4399049405.kf1: Critical intersectional conversations on race/sexuality/gender occurred infrequently in GSAs; youth desired them and wanted advisors to facilitate critically reflective discussions.

https://openalex.org/W4395465598.kf1: A developmental antecedent was associated with later adherence to masculinity norms, significant only for boys.

https://openalex.org/W4401178358.demographics: Children of low-income parents (40% Black, 33% mixed/other)
https://openalex.org/W4401178358.kf1: Adding parent education/workforce training to Head Start neither improved nor worsened children's academic, language, math, or inhibitory-control outcomes over three years.

https://openalex.org/W4401396166.teacher_detail: Support for engaged/playful learning (exploration, social connectedness, emotional climate)
https://openalex.org/W4401396166.kf1: The PLAY observational tool captured three classroom domains of engaged learning; a teacher professional-development program had large impacts on all three (d=0.55-1.01).

https://openalex.org/W4399124305.kf1: Chinese parents believed fathers steer 'broad' child development and masculinity cultivation while mothers handle 'trivial' tasks, reflecting pressure around rigid masculinity standards.

https://openalex.org/W4404012494.demographics: Straight and sexual/gender-minority Black, Latinx, and white youth
https://openalex.org/W4404012494.kf1: Critical-consciousness measures showed separate reflection and political-efficacy factors with partial invariance across groups; political efficacy related to better mental health and grades across groups.

https://openalex.org/W4393864876.kf1: A new active-listening measure (open-ended and follow-up questions) was reliable and sensitive to intervention change; follow-up questions related to empathy and being perceived as a good listener.

https://openalex.org/W4396238336.demographics: Children experiencing violence in the home (INSPIRE strategies)
https://openalex.org/W4396238336.kf1: Found extensive evidence for parent/caregiver-support interventions to prevent violence against children, but scarce evidence on other INSPIRE strategies and underrepresentation of LMIC/conflict-affected samples.

https://openalex.org/W4392058380.kf1: Presents a bioecological/dynamic-systems framework for how acute and chronic climate-change risks experienced prenatally and early in life threaten human development.

https://openalex.org/W4405564842.kf1: After four years, monthly unconditional cash transfers showed no significant impacts on children's language, executive function, social-emotional, or brain-activity outcomes.
https://openalex.org/W4405564842.effect_sizes: No significant impacts on primary outcomes

https://openalex.org/W4401760009.kf1: No statistically detectable impacts of the high-cash gift on maternal reports of children's language, socioemotional development, or developmental-delay concerns.

https://openalex.org/W4405587357.kf1: Four years of monthly cash transfers had no impact on the primary brain-activity index; an exploratory analysis showed higher alpha power in the high-cash group, needing replication.
https://openalex.org/W4405587357.effect_sizes: Higher alpha power in high-cash group (exploratory)

https://openalex.org/W4398194297.kf1: Cash transfers did not change breastfeeding, child-care use, or employment overall, but delayed child-care start by ~1 month and increased mothers' ability to meet breastfeeding intentions.

https://openalex.org/W4408939972.kf1: Cash transfers were associated with a trend of increased pregnancy after three years but no impacts on births, miscarriages, or terminations.

https://openalex.org/W4411343763.kf1: After four years, no statistically detectable differences in maternal depression, anxiety, or BMI, or in child health/BMI between cash groups.

https://openalex.org/W4414494792.demographics: Undocumented, unaccompanied, and citizen children of undocumented immigrant parents
https://openalex.org/W4414494792.kf1: Convening of scholars and providers established consensus areas and gaps to set a research agenda on immigrant children affected by undocumented status.

https://openalex.org/W4407028715.kf1: Identified five stakeholder-varying conceptualisations of SEL in conflict-affected Diffa, shaped by conflict, trauma, psychosocial support, poverty, and religion.

https://openalex.org/W4413118124.demographics: Mothers below the federal poverty threshold
https://openalex.org/W4413118124.kf1: Cash transfers moderately increased income and reduced poverty but did not improve subjective economic hardship or most maternal well-being measures; high-cash mothers engaged more in enriching child activities.
https://openalex.org/W4413118124.effect_sizes: Moderate income increase; mostly non-significant well-being effects

https://openalex.org/W4412853744.kf1: Introduces LEVANTE, a global framework for coordinated, longitudinal data collection to measure variability in children's learning and development (literacy, numeracy, cognitive/social constructs).

https://openalex.org/W4412407329.kf1: Greater advocacy involvement and inclusive school policies were associated with higher self-worth among LGBTQ+ youth, with a GSA-level contextual effect beyond individual advocacy.

https://openalex.org/W4416995156.sample_size: Adolescents with 247 mothers and 191 fathers (Year 10 and Year 15)
https://openalex.org/W4416995156.kf1: Maternal supportive emotion socialization predicted girls' increased self-esteem and decreased depressive symptoms over time; paternal emotion socialization was inversely linked to boys' depressive symptoms.

https://openalex.org/W4410311272.kf1: Validated a new outness measure for sexual and gender minority youth, confirming it accurately assesses sexual- and gender-identity outness and supports subgroup comparisons.

https://openalex.org/W4410580585.kf1: Reviews individual-level psychological acculturation, distinguishing it from group-level acculturation and outlining principles, processes, and future directions.

https://openalex.org/W4410580756.kf1: Reviews individual-level psychological acculturation, distinguishing it from group-level acculturation (duplicate record).
